LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. CROSS-DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. (CCP 430.10(E).) MS. POLAKOVIC HAS PLED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ALLEGE VIOLATIONS OF THE FAIR DEBT COLLECTION PRACTICES ACT (FDCPA) AND THE ROSENTHAL DEBT COLLECTION PRACTICES ACT. THE ALLEGATIONS ARE NOT CLEARLY BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS ON THE FACE OF THE CROSS-COMPLAINT. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S OTHER ARGUMENTS INVOLVE QUESTIONS OF FACT, THAT ARE NOT SUBJECT TO DEMURRER. CROSS-DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS DENIED. CROSS-DEFENDANT DID NOT CITE TO ANY CALIFORNIA LEGAL AUTHORITY IN SUPPORT OF ITS REQUEST. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT ; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976

NTC OF MTN AND MTN TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY DEFENDANT POLAKOVIC, ALEXANDRA HEARING SET FOR AUG-30-2013 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF FAILS TO MEET ITS BURDEN TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT WAS PERSONALLY SERVED ON JULY 7, 2013. THE RETURN OF THE PROCESS SERVER ESTABLISHES A PRESUMPTION, AFFECTING THE BURDEN OF PRODUCING EVIDENCE. EVIDENCE CODE 647. HOWEVER, HERE, DEFENDANT DECLARES UNDER PENALTY OF PERJURY THAT SHE WAS NOT HOME FROM JULY 6 TO JULY 14, 2013 DURING WHICH TIME THE SUMMONS WAS ALLEGEDLY SERVED ON HER PERSONALLY. IT IS NOT CLEAR WHETHER THE PROOF OF SERVICE WAS SIGNED (THE COPY ATTACHED TO THE OPPOSITION HAS ONLY "S" ON THE SIGNATURE LINE). THERE IS NO DECLARATION FROM THE PROCESS SERVER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-25-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES A . MICHEL, TEL. NO. 239-0156 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS-DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is Overruled. (CCP 430.10(e).) Ms. Polakovic has pled sufficient facts to allege violations of the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act (FDCPA) and the Rosenthal Debt Collection Practices Act. The allegations are not clearly barred by the statute of limitations on the face of the Cross-Complaint. Cross-Defendant's other arguments involve questions of fact, that are not subject to demurrer. Cross-Defendant's request for judicial notice is denied. Cross-Defendant did not cite to any California legal authority in support of its request. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert ; Court Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Continued To Feb-25-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. CROSS DEFENDANT PATENAUDE & FELIX, APC'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is continued on the Court's own motion to February 25, 2014. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Sep-17-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
James A. Michel, Esq. of the Law Offices of James A. Michel, Tel. 415.951.4949, for defendant and moving party;
No other appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted the tentative ruling which was uncontested. Plaintiff fails to meet its burden to show that Defendant was personally served on July 7, 2013. The return of the process server establishes a presumption, affecting the burden of producing evidence. Evidence Code 647. However, here, Defendant declares under penalty of perjury that she was not home from July 6 to July 14, 2013 during which time the summons was allegedly served on her personally. It is not clear whether the proof of service was signed (the copy attached to the opposition has only "S" on the signature line). There is no declaration from the process server. Order signed. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Aug-30-2013 Continued To Sep-17-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ALEXANDRA POLAKOVIC'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons is continued to September 17, 2013 to give opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 30, 2013 at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving papers. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
